county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Frederick County formed 1748 from Prince George's County. County seat is Frederick. Once encompassed all of western Maryland before Allegany, Washington, Carroll, Howard, and Montgomery were carved from it. Heavy German settlement in the Monocacy Valley. Key Civil War location (Battle of Monocacy, 1864). Named for Frederick Calvert, 6th Baron Baltimore.
county_metadata.research_tips: MSA and Frederick County Circuit Court (Frederick). Formed 1748 from Prince George's County — once encompassed all of western Maryland. Land records on mdlandrec.net from 1748. The Historical Society of Frederick County has extensive genealogical collections including family files, church record transcriptions, and cemetery records. Heavy German settlement in the Monocacy Valley — German Reformed, Lutheran, and Moravian church records are essential. The Evangelical Reformed Church records (now United Church of Christ) date from the 1740s. Frederick was a Civil War crossroads — Battle of Monocacy (1864) and Antietam campaign records. Check MSA for colonial land patents and the Maryland Room at the C. Burr Artz Library for local resources.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Frederick land records on mdlandrec.net; MSA has digitized deed books, mortgages, and land patents; one of the most complete online land record systems in the US

source_profiles.repository: Historical Society of Frederick County / Maryland State Archives / Local parishes
source_profiles.coverage_notes: German Reformed (now UCC), Lutheran, and Moravian church records are essential for Frederick County's large German-descent population; Evangelical Reformed Church records from the 1740s; Catholic records from St. John's (1763). The Historical Society has transcriptions

source_profiles.source_name: Frederick County Civil War Records -- Antietam / Monocacy
source_profiles.repository: NARA / Maryland State Archives / Monocacy National Battlefield
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Frederick County was a Civil War crossroads — Battle of Monocacy (1864) and proximity to Antietam (1862). Military records for Frederick County soldiers who served in both Union and Confederate forces; battlefield records at NARA and NPS

source_profiles.repository: Historical Society of Frederick County / Library of Congress
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Frederick newspapers from the 1790s including the Frederick-Town Herald and Frederick Examiner; legal notices, land sales, runaway slave advertisements, and community news; the Maryland Room at C. Burr Artz Library has newspaper indexes

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Garrett County formed 1872 from Allegany County — the last county created in Maryland. County seat is Oakland. Maryland's westernmost and most mountainous county. Economy based on coal, timber, and railroads. Named for John W. Garrett, president of the B&O Railroad.
county_metadata.research_tips: MSA and Garrett County Circuit Court (Oakland). Maryland's westernmost county, formed 1872 from Allegany — the last county created in Maryland. Pre-1872 records are in Allegany County. Land records on mdlandrec.net from 1872. Mountainous terrain with coal mining, timber, and railroad industries. B&O Railroad records are important for tracing families. Strong Scots-Irish and German heritage. The Garrett County Historical Society in Oakland maintains local records. Many families had connections across the border into West Virginia and Pennsylvania. Ruth Enlow Library of Garrett County holds local history collections. Check Garrett County deeds carefully as the county's creation required resurveying many boundaries.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Garrett County land records on mdlandrec.net from 1872 (county formation); pre-1872 records under Allegany County; MSA has digitized all Garrett County deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Harford County formed 1774 from Baltimore County. County seat is Bel Air. Located in northeastern Maryland along the Chesapeake Bay. Havre de Grace at the mouth of the Susquehanna was an early transportation hub. Named for Henry Harford, the last proprietary governor of Maryland (illegitimate son of Frederick Calvert).
county_metadata.research_tips: MSA and Harford County Circuit Court (Bel Air). Formed 1774 from Baltimore County — check Baltimore County for pre-1774 records. Land records on mdlandrec.net from 1774. The Historical Society of Harford County in Bel Air holds extensive local records, family files, and church record transcriptions. Harford County had both Quaker (Deer Creek Meeting) and Anglican settlement. The Aberdeen Proving Ground (1917) generated military records. Havre de Grace at the mouth of the Susquehanna River was a transportation hub. James Rumsey's steamboat experiments and the Susquehanna canal system brought workers. Check MSA for pre-Revolution Bush River Lower Hundred records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Harford land records on mdlandrec.net; MSA has digitized deed books, mortgages, and land patents; one of the most complete online land record systems in the US

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Howard County formed 1851 (previously Howard District of Anne Arundel County). County seat is Ellicott City. Located between Baltimore and Washington. Named for John Eager Howard, Revolutionary War hero and Maryland governor. The Ellicott brothers (Quakers from Pennsylvania) founded Ellicott's Mills in 1772.
county_metadata.research_tips: MSA and Howard County Circuit Court (Ellicott City). Formed 1851 as Howard District of Anne Arundel County (became a full county). Pre-1851 records are at Anne Arundel County. Land records on mdlandrec.net from 1840. The Howard County Historical Society in Ellicott City has local records and family files. Ellicott City (founded 1772 by Quaker Ellicott brothers) has mill and industrial records. The county was agricultural with tobacco and grain. Significant enslaved population documented in Anne Arundel County records pre-1851 and Howard County records after. Patapsco River valley records document early industrial families. B&O Railroad's first terminus was at Ellicott's Mills (1830) — railroad worker records may be relevant.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Howard land records on mdlandrec.net; MSA has digitized deed books, mortgages, and land patents; one of the most complete online land record systems in the US

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Kent County formed 1642 as one of Maryland's original counties, the oldest on the Eastern Shore. County seat is Chestertown, home of Washington College (1782). Agricultural county with tobacco and grain. Named for the English county of Kent.
county_metadata.research_tips: MSA and Kent County Circuit Court (Chestertown). One of Maryland's original counties (1642), the oldest on the Eastern Shore. Land records on mdlandrec.net from the 1640s — among the oldest in the state. Washington College in Chestertown (founded 1782 with George Washington's support) — college archives may have local family connections. Kent County was a tobacco and grain county with significant enslaved population. Strong Quaker presence — check meeting records. The Kent County Historical Society holds local genealogical resources. Shrewsbury Church (Episcopal, 1692) has colonial parish records. Kent County records at MSA include colonial court proceedings from the 1640s. Check for Quaker manumission records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Kent land records on mdlandrec.net; MSA has digitized deed books, mortgages, and land patents; one of the most complete online land record systems in the US

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Montgomery County formed 1776 from Frederick County during the Revolution. County seat is Rockville. Located adjacent to Washington, DC. Historically agricultural (tobacco, then diversified farming) until 20th-century suburban development. Named for General Richard Montgomery, killed at the Battle of Quebec (1775).
county_metadata.research_tips: MSA and Montgomery County Circuit Court (Rockville). Formed 1776 from Frederick County. Land records on mdlandrec.net from 1776. Montgomery County Historical Society in Rockville holds extensive local records including family files, photographs, and cemetery transcriptions. The county was agricultural (tobacco, then diversified farming) until suburban development in the mid-20th century. Significant Quaker settlement at Sandy Spring — Sandy Spring Museum and Friends Meeting records are important sources. Brookeville served briefly as the US capital during the War of 1812 (one day, August 26, 1814). Large enslaved population documented in county records. NIH (1938) and other federal facilities brought diverse population after WWII.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Montgomery land records on mdlandrec.net; MSA has digitized deed books, mortgages, and land patents; one of the most complete online land record systems in the US

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Prince George's County formed 1696 from Charles and Calvert counties. County seat is Upper Marlboro. Located adjacent to Washington, DC on the south. Major tobacco county with one of the largest enslaved populations in Maryland. Named for Prince George of Denmark, husband of Queen Anne.
county_metadata.research_tips: MSA and Prince George's County Circuit Court (Upper Marlboro). Formed 1696 from Charles and Calvert counties. Land records on mdlandrec.net from the 1690s. Prince George's County once encompassed most of central and western Maryland. Tobacco plantation county with one of the largest enslaved populations in Maryland — extensive slave records at MSA including inventories, sales, and manumissions. The Prince George's County Historical Society holds local records. Bowie State University (founded 1865 as a school for African Americans) archives contain African American community records. Catholic and Anglican parish records are important. Marietta House Museum and Riversdale House Museum preserve plantation-era documents.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Prince George's land records on mdlandrec.net; MSA has digitized deed books, mortgages, and land patents; one of the most complete online land record systems in the US

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Queen Anne's County formed 1706 from Talbot, Kent, and Dorchester counties. County seat is Centreville. Eastern Shore agricultural county. Named for Queen Anne of Great Britain. Wye House plantation (Lloyd family) is historically significant — Frederick Douglass describes it in his autobiography.
county_metadata.research_tips: MSA and Queen Anne's County Circuit Court (Centreville). Formed 1706 from Talbot, Kent, and Dorchester counties — check parent counties for earlier records. Land records on mdlandrec.net from 1706. Eastern Shore county with tobacco and grain agriculture. The Queen Anne's County Historical Society and the county free library hold local genealogical resources. Strong Anglican/Episcopal parish records from 1692 (St. Paul's, Centreville). Significant enslaved population — estate inventories at MSA list enslaved persons. The county was named for Queen Anne of Great Britain. Wye House plantation records (Lloyd family) at MSA and the Maryland Historical Society are important for enslaved genealogy — Frederick Douglass references Wye House in his autobiography.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Queen Anne's land records on mdlandrec.net; MSA has digitized deed books, mortgages, and land patents; one of the most complete online land record systems in the US

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Somerset County formed 1666 as one of the original Maryland counties on the Lower Eastern Shore. County seat is Princess Anne. Originally much larger before Worcester (1742) and Wicomico (1867) were carved from it. Named for Mary Somerset, wife of the proprietor's sister.
county_metadata.research_tips: MSA and Somerset County Circuit Court (Princess Anne). One of the original counties (1666) on the Lower Eastern Shore. Land records on mdlandrec.net from the 1660s. Somerset County was once much larger, before Wicomico (1867) and Worcester (1742) were carved from it. Agricultural county with tobacco, grain, and seafood industries. Significant enslaved population and large free Black community — check MSA for free negro certificates and manumission records. The Princess Anne area has important African American history — the University of Maryland Eastern Shore (founded 1886) archives hold community records. Manokin Presbyterian Church (1672) has early colonial records. Somerset County Historical Society maintains local genealogical files.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Somerset land records on mdlandrec.net; MSA has digitized deed books, mortgages, and land patents; one of the most complete online land record systems in the US

source_profiles.repository: Maryland State Archives, Annapolis
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Somerset County had a significant enslaved population and growing free Black community; manumission records, free negro certificates, and estate inventories at MSA; University of Maryland Eastern Shore (founded 1886) archives hold community records

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: St. Mary's County formed 1637 as the first county in Maryland, site of the colony's founding at St. Mary's City (1634). County seat is Leonardtown. Southern Maryland county on the western shore. St. Mary's City was the colonial capital until 1694. Maryland's founding as a Catholic colony makes St. Mary's records especially significant for Catholic genealogy.
county_metadata.research_tips: MSA and St. Mary's County Circuit Court (Leonardtown). The first county in Maryland (1637) and the site of Maryland's founding at St. Mary's City (1634). Land records on mdlandrec.net from the 1630s — the oldest in Maryland. Historic St. Mary's City archaeological and historical records are invaluable for colonial research. The St. Mary's County Historical Society holds local records. Catholic records are paramount — the first Catholic mass in English-speaking America was celebrated here. Jesuit records at Georgetown University document early Catholic families and enslaved persons on Jesuit plantations. Tobacco plantation economy with extensive enslaved population records. St. Mary's was the colonial capital until 1694 when it moved to Annapolis. Check MSA for the richest colonial Maryland records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: St. Mary's land records on mdlandrec.net; MSA has digitized deed books, mortgages, and land patents; one of the most complete online land record systems in the US

source_profiles.source_name: St. Mary's Land Records -- Maryland State Archives / mdlandrec.net
source_profiles.repository: Maryland State Archives, Annapolis / mdlandrec.net
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Land records for St. Mary's on mdlandrec.net; digitized deed books, mortgages, and land patents; MSA has indexed and imaged virtually all Maryland land records online through mdlandrec.net, one of the best state land record systems in the US

source_profiles.source_name: St. Mary's Probate Records -- MSA / Register of Wills / Orphans' Court
source_profiles.repository: Maryland State Archives, Annapolis / St. Mary's Register of Wills
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, inventories, administrations, and estate accounts for St. Mary's; Orphans' Court handled probate, guardianships, and apprenticeships; records at MSA and county Register of Wills

source_profiles.source_name: St. Mary's Federal Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1790-1950 for St. Mary's; Maryland was enumerated in every federal census; 1790 census is complete for Maryland; slave schedules available for 1850 and 1860

source_profiles.source_name: St. Mary's Marriage Records -- MSA / Clerk of Circuit Court
source_profiles.repository: Maryland State Archives, Annapolis / St. Mary's Clerk of Circuit Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and records for St. Mary's; earlier marriages found in church records; FamilySearch has indexed many Maryland marriages; MSA holds county marriage records

source_profiles.source_name: St. Mary's County Catholic and Church Records -- Jesuit / Catholic Archives
source_profiles.repository: Georgetown University Archives / Archdiocese of Baltimore / Historic St. Mary's City
source_profiles.coverage_notes: The first Catholic mass in English-speaking America was celebrated at St. Mary's (1634); Jesuit records from the earliest Maryland settlement; parish records including St. Francis Xavier (1640s); Georgetown University Archives hold Jesuit plantation records documenting enslaved persons

source_profiles.source_name: St. Mary's County Enslaved Persons Records -- MSA / Georgetown University
source_profiles.repository: Maryland State Archives / Georgetown University Archives / Historic St. Mary's City
source_profiles.coverage_notes: St. Mary's County had a large enslaved population on tobacco plantations and Jesuit estates; the 1838 Jesuit slave sale of 272 persons is documented at Georgetown; estate inventories, manumission records, and free negro certificates at MSA

source_profiles.source_name: St. Mary's County Colonial Court Records -- MSA / Historic St. Mary's City
source_profiles.repository: Maryland State Archives / Historic St. Mary's City
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Among the oldest court records in Maryland; colonial provincial and county court proceedings from the 1630s; MSA holds originals; Historic St. Mary's City Commission has archaeological and documentary resources for the colonial capital period
